channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: "Enjoy My Pregnancy Fashion": Usha Vance On Her $8 Maternity Dress Analysis

media:content.media:description: US Second Lady Usha Vance was seen wearing coral maternity dress from Old Navy.

item.description: Usha, who is pregnant with her fourth child, was featured in a New York Times fashion article titled "The Politics and Power of the Pregnancy Image."
item.content:encoded: Usha, who is pregnant with her fourth child, was featured in a New York Times fashion article titled "The Politics and Power of the Pregnancy Image."

item.title: Bangladesh PM, Chinese Water Minister Discuss Teesta Plan Implementation

media:content.media:description: Bangladesh PM sought support of Chinese government in Bangladesh's water resources management

item.description: Bangladesh and China agree to boost cooperation on river management including Teesta project
item.content:encoded: Bangladesh and China agree to boost cooperation on river management including Teesta project

item.title: 'Mad As A Murder Hornet' Trump Squabbles With Disaffected Senate

media:content.media:description: Donald Trump and US Senate Republicans speak to media at the US Capitol.

item.description: US Senator John Kennedy, a Louisiana Republican, told reporters that Trump was "mad as a murder hornet" at the war powers vote because it undermined his ongoing negotiations with Iran.
item.content:encoded: US Senator John Kennedy, a Louisiana Republican, told reporters that Trump was "mad as a murder hornet" at the war powers vote because it undermined his ongoing negotiations with Iran.

item.title: US Soldiers Say Injuries From Iran War Were More Serious Than Army Reported

media:content.media:description: Army officials said families may be misunderstanding military terms such as "not seriously injured" .

item.description: The allegations came after Hegseth said that nearly 90% of the 400 American service members injured during the conflict with Iran had suffered only minor injuries and had returned to duty.
item.content:encoded: The allegations came after Hegseth said that nearly 90% of the 400 American service members injured during the conflict with Iran had suffered only minor injuries and had returned to duty.

item.title: Explained: What Makes Venezuela Prone To Devastating Earthquakes

media:content.media:description: Emergency services work at the site of a collapsed building after an earthquake, in Caracas, Venezuela.

item.description: Many earthquakes in Venezuela are "shallow-focus," meaning they occur less than 15 to 20 kilometres below the ground.
item.content:encoded: Many earthquakes in Venezuela are "shallow-focus," meaning they occur less than 15 to 20 kilometres below the ground.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquakes Live: Venezuela's 7.5 Earthquake Is Most Powerful To Hit Country In Over A Century

media:content.media:description: Venezuela Earthquake Live: The first earthquake had a magnitude of 7.2

item.description: 7.5 Magnitude Earthquake hit Venezuela Live Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.
item.content:encoded: 7.5 Magnitude Earthquake hit Venezuela Live Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.

item.title: "Nobody's Laughing At Us Anymore": Trump Opens US 250th Birthday Party

media:content.media:description: Donald Trump hosted a rally on Washington's National Mall

item.description: Surprisingly, Trump wrapped his speech in less than half an hour - making it one of the shortest rally addresses of his second term and perhaps his entire political career.
item.content:encoded: Surprisingly, Trump wrapped his speech in less than half an hour - making it one of the shortest rally addresses of his second term and perhaps his entire political career.

item.title: Chinese Man Caught Breeding 300 Pythons In Flat After Power Bill Shock

media:content.media:description: Pythons are classified as Grade Two protected animals in China.

item.description: An unemployed man in China has been jailed after police investigated his flat's unusually high electricity bills, only to discover he was running a massive, illegal breeding operation containing more than 300 protected pythons.
item.content:encoded: An unemployed man in China has been jailed after police investigated his flat's unusually high electricity bills, only to discover he was running a massive, illegal breeding operation containing more than 300 protected pythons.

item.title: Europe Becomes World's Fastest-Warming Continent As Heatwaves Disrupt Daily Life

media:content.media:description: The consequences of this accelerating crisis are becoming dangerous.

item.description: A recent report confirms that Europe is warming twice as fast as the global average, triggering severe heatwaves, environmental damage, and public health red alerts across major nations.
item.content:encoded: A recent report confirms that Europe is warming twice as fast as the global average, triggering severe heatwaves, environmental damage, and public health red alerts across major nations.

item.title: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba Of "Illicitly" Accessing AI Models

media:content.media:description: Anthropic claimed that the effort involved 28.8 million exchanges

item.description: Anthropic claimed that a campaign by operators linked to Alibaba's Qwen AI lab targeted Claude's most prized capabilities, including software engineering and agentic reasoning.
item.content:encoded: Anthropic claimed that a campaign by operators linked to Alibaba's Qwen AI lab targeted Claude's most prized capabilities, including software engineering and agentic reasoning.

item.title: Twin Quakes Rock Venezuela: Top 10 Deadliest Earthquakes On Record

media:content.media:description: Buildings collapsed in and around Caracas, the capital city which remains the worst affected.

item.description: Amid the devastating incident, Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodriguez has declared a state of emergency.
item.content:encoded: Amid the devastating incident, Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodriguez has declared a state of emergency.

item.title: "We Need Flashlights": Search On For Survivors After Venezuela Earthquakes

media:content.media:description: Buildings across the city were destroyed.

item.description: Neighbours looked on helplessly at the home's remains as one of them clambered around the debris, listening out for responses but hearing only silence.
item.content:encoded: Neighbours looked on helplessly at the home's remains as one of them clambered around the debris, listening out for responses but hearing only silence.

item.title: Europe's Summers Are Heating Up, But Air Conditioners Are Still Rare

media:content.media:description: Europe is baking under a scorching heatwave.

item.description: According to the International Energy Agency (IEA), nearly 90% of US homes have air conditioning, compared with only around 20% across Europe.
item.content:encoded: According to the International Energy Agency (IEA), nearly 90% of US homes have air conditioning, compared with only around 20% across Europe.

item.title: UN Report Highlights Severe Impact of Gaza Conflict On Palestinian Children

media:content.media:description: UN inquiry documents severe physical and psychological harm to Gaza children.

item.description: UN report reveals high child casualties and trauma in Gaza conflict, urging international action.
item.content:encoded: UN report reveals high child casualties and trauma in Gaza conflict, urging international action.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Iran War: Has New Delhi Lost Out, And Has Pak Really Gained? By Shashi Tharoor
item.description: To imagine a Nobel Peace for the terrorist-sponsoring Field Marshal Asim Munir may be a fantasy too far. The world sees clearly the difference between an autonomous actor and a message-carrying stool-pigeon.
item.content:encoded: To imagine a Nobel Peace for the terrorist-sponsoring Field Marshal Asim Munir may be a fantasy too far. The world sees clearly the difference between an autonomous actor and a message-carrying stool-pigeon.

item.title: Gen Z In UK Are Earning More Than Millennials In Early Stages Of Career

media:content.media:description: Gen Z is benefiting, financially at least, from more young people living at home with their parents.

item.description: The career prospects of Generation Z have become a political concern after youth unemployment rose to its highest in over a decade, with more than a million 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.
item.content:encoded: The career prospects of Generation Z have become a political concern after youth unemployment rose to its highest in over a decade, with more than a million 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

item.title: War Games Show Sea Drones Are Key To Defending Taiwan, Japan Against China

media:content.media:description: Building and operating large fleets of sea drones poses challenges.

item.description: Just as the wars in Ukraine and the Middle East have laid bare the value of cheap airborne drones, sea drones are seen as playing a crucial role in the Indo-Pacific region.
item.content:encoded: Just as the wars in Ukraine and the Middle East have laid bare the value of cheap airborne drones, sea drones are seen as playing a crucial role in the Indo-Pacific region.

item.title: Venezuela Hit By Twin Earthquakes: A Look At Most Powerful Ones In History

media:content.media:description: At least 32 people died and more than 700 suffered injuries in Venezuela earthquakes

item.description: Twin earthquakes measuring 7.2 and 7.5 struck Venezuela within a minute on Wednesday evening.
item.content:encoded: Twin earthquakes measuring 7.2 and 7.5 struck Venezuela within a minute on Wednesday evening.

item.title: Buildings Toppled, People Trapped: Pics Show Damage As Quakes Hit Venezuela

media:content.media:description: Venezuela Earthquake: Powerful tremors hit Caracas region during a public holiday.

item.description: Venezuela Earthquake: Twin strong earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 hit west of Caracas causing building collapses and injuries
item.content:encoded: Venezuela Earthquake: Twin strong earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 hit west of Caracas causing building collapses and injuries

item.title: "Don't Even Know How Long It Lasted": Venezuela Residents Recount Earthquake

media:content.media:description: The earthquakes triggered panic in Caracas and other Venezuelan states

item.description: Back-to-back powerful earthquakes of 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening.
item.content:encoded: Back-to-back powerful earthquakes of 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening.
